title: hieracium melanops subsp. subadusticeps
intro: Hieracium melanops subsp. subadusticeps, also known as a type of hawkweed, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family Asteraceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe and protect the skin from infections. The health benefits of Hieracium melanops subsp. subadusticeps include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its possible use as an antiseptic and a treatment for certain skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
